data.body:
## Description

**What**: Modify /dso:sprint dispatch logic to detect documentation update stories and route them to the dso:doc-writer agent instead of the generic implementation agent.
**Why**: Without sprint integration, the doc-writer agent exists but is never invoked during normal epic execution. This story connects the agent to the automated workflow.
**Scope**:
- IN: Sprint SKILL.md dispatch modification; title pattern detection ("Update project docs to reflect [epic summary]"); subagent_type dispatch to dso:doc-writer
- OUT: Agent creation (Story 9e22-2d3d); standalone skill (Story f9e4-4b68)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, /dso:sprint identifies documentation update stories by title pattern and dispatches them to dso:doc-writer via subagent_type instead of the generic implementation agent
  ← Satisfies: "can be dispatched by /dso:sprint when processing a documentation update story"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Sprint dispatch modification touches safeguarded skill file (plugins/dso/skills/sprint/) — requires user approval per CLAUDE.md rule 18
- [Testing] tests/skills/test_agent_dispatch_validation.py validates that sprint/SKILL.md references the correct named agents. After modifying sprint dispatch logic, add a test case verifying the sprint skill references dso:doc-writer for documentation story dispatch.
- [Reliability] The sprint dispatch detects doc update stories by title pattern. If preplanning generates a slightly different title, the doc-writer agent will not be dispatched and the story silently falls through to generic dispatch. Consider whether the dispatch should also check for a ticket label/tag as secondary signal.
